sections.name: D
sections.title: SECTION D: OPEN QUESTIONS (5 Questions — 3 marks each)
sections.description: Answer the following questions in full sentences.

questions.text: Explain why keyboard shortcuts are important when developing a website. Give TWO examples of shortcuts and what they do.
questions.correctAnswer: Keyboard shortcuts are important in website development because they save time and improve efficiency, allowing developers to work faster without switching between the mouse and keyboard. Two examples are: Ctrl + C — Copies selected text or content; Ctrl + S — Saves the current file.

questions.text: Describe the basic structure of an HTML webpage. Name and explain the purpose of THREE key HTML tags used in the structure.
questions.correctAnswer: An HTML webpage has a basic structure made up of three main tags: <html> — The root tag that wraps the entire webpage content; <head> — Contains meta information about the page such as the title and links to CSS files; <body> — Contains all the visible content that appears on the webpage, such as text, images, and links.

questions.text: What is the difference between margin and padding in CSS? Give an example of when you would use each one.
questions.correctAnswer: Padding is the space between the content of an element and its border. For example, you would use padding to add space inside a button so the text does not touch the edges. Margin is the space outside the element's border, separating it from other elements. For example, you would use margin to create space between two paragraphs or between a heading and an image.

questions.text: Explain TWO ways you can apply CSS styles to an HTML webpage.
questions.correctAnswer: Internal CSS — Written inside a <style> tag placed within the <head> section of the HTML file. This applies styles only to that specific page. External CSS — Written in a separate .css file and linked to the HTML page using the <link> tag in the <head> section. This allows the same styles to be applied to multiple pages.

questions.text: A student wants to create a simple webpage with a heading, a paragraph, and a link to another website. Write the basic HTML code they would need and briefly explain what each line does.
questions.correctAnswer:
Welcome to My Website

This is my first paragraph of text.

Click here to visit Google
